_comment: THE ATTESTATION LEDGER — the single, committed record of every human clinical attestation that
permits a rule or candidate to claim source-supported grounding via `sourcePassageId`.
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS (reviewer gate, third pass, 2026-07-21): the attestation records used to
live as constants INSIDE the backfill scripts. That made the gate generator-side only — a
direct hand-edit of modules/anemia/rules.json could point a rule at a clean source-supported
passage and `npm run validate` exited 0, because the validator never consulted any attestation
record. The reviewer demonstrated exactly that. Moving the ledger here lets BOTH the generator
and scripts/validate-kb.mjs read the same source of truth, so the committed data is checked,
not merely the code path that usually produces it.
Both lists ship EMPTY, and that is the honest state: no credentialed human clinician has
attested any rule-to-passage or candidate-to-passage mapping in this knowledge base. While they
are empty, ANY source-supported binding found in rules.json or candidates.json is a validation
ERROR, regardless of how it got there.
Entry shape: { ruleId | candidateId, passageId, attestedBy, credential, attestedOn,
attestationRef }. Every field is a required non-empty string. `attestedBy` should name a real
reviewer. NOTE: this check is a tripwire, not proof — code cannot establish that an
attester is human; see docs/attestations/README.md. It is rejected if it looks like a model,
agent, council, or pipeline
identifier. `passageId` must resolve to a passage that is currently bindable (not quarantined
by the EP3-T5 fidelity audit), and must belong to a source the entity actually cites.
